name: Mama's TOO!
tip: Order the Angry Nonna (hot soppressata & hot honey) or Cacio e Pepe (4 cheeses and cracked black pepper) slice for their signature thick-crust Detroit-style pizza.

name: Ceres
tip: They only sell pizzas by the whole pie, so bring friends — must-try is the Nduja pizza with creamy burrata and aged balsamic, or the mushroom pizza with crème fraiche and onion.

name: Cello's Pizzeria
tip: Order the burrata slice — crispy crust with fresh tomato sauce, garlic, and creamy burrata on top.

name: Fini Pizza
tip: Try the hot long peppers and shallot slice for the standout flavour combination at this thin-slice spot.

name: L'industrie
tip: Don't miss the fig jam and bacon slice — they have locations in both West Village and Williamsburg.

name: Ace's Pizza
tip: Order the Spicy Boy for a Detroit-style thick crust topped with hot honey and burrata.

name: Una Pizza Napoletana
tip: Known for fluffy Neapolitan-style pies with ultra-fresh tomato sauce — expect whole pies only, not slices.

name: Rubirosa
tip: Famous for their tie-dye pizza drizzled with pesto and vodka sauce — go for the sit-down experience and expect a wait.

name: Diamond Slice
tip: An underrated slice shop with creative flavours like Elote and French Onion available in both thin crispy and thick square cuts.

name: Radio Bakery NYC
tip: Their sourdough tomato focaccia hits the counter every day at 10am — arrive early before it sells out.

name: Not A Normal Market
tip: Located at One Hanson Place in Brooklyn, this pop-up vintage market sells authentic designer bags, accessories, and vintage clothing.

name: Brooklyn Flea (Dumbo)
tip: Open every Saturday and Sunday 10am–5pm year-round, making it a reliable weekend activity in NYC.

name: Moma Design Store
tip: Located at 44 W 53rd St, this store stocks creative novelty gifts like food-themed socks by Eat My Socks alongside design objects and Jellycat plushies.

name: Moma Design Store x Jellycat
tip: The Jellycat pop-up at the Moma Design Store (44 W 53rd St) was open until March 29, with a second location at 81 Spring St in SoHo.

name: Nowon
tip: Located at 507 E 6th St, Nowon is praised as one of the best value spots in NYC and is also a great option for birthday dinners.

name: Summit One Vanderbilt Observatory
tip: The caption tags this as the location and says the sunset view from here is 'just unreal'.

name: Roosevelt Island Tram
tip: You can ride this cable car between Manhattan and Roosevelt Island for just $3.90 using a MetroCard, with spectacular city views.

name: Dim Sum Sam
tip: Their signature Shanghai Juicy Pork Dumplings (xiao long bao) are the must-order item — bite carefully to avoid the hot broth.

name: Greenwich Letterpress
tip: A charming stationery shop in the West Village known for letterpress cards and paper goods, perfect for unique souvenirs.

name: East Village Postal
tip: A neighbourhood stationery and postal goods store in the East Village worth visiting for notebook lovers.

name: Cornershop Hightide
tip: A Brooklyn stationery store stocking the popular Japanese Hightide brand alongside other curated paper goods.

name: Goods For The Study
tip: A well-curated stationery and office supply shop with a broad selection of pens, notebooks, and desk accessories.

name: Yoseka Stationery
tip: A beloved Japanese-style stationery shop with an impressive selection of fountain pens and writing supplies.

name: The Analog Stationer
tip: Specialises in high-end pens and analog writing tools — ideal for fine-point pen enthusiasts.

name: Mogutable
tip: A charming trinket shop selling teapots, ceramics, tote bags, candles and matcha goods — great for unique gifts.

name: Loaf on Paper
tip: A charming stationery and craft supply shop stocked with sticker sheets, rubber stamps, washi tape, and journaling supplies.

name: Bungalow
tip: Highly recommended Indian restaurant in NYC known for attentive tableside service.

name: Dhamaka
tip: A favourite for its house-made paneer described as buttery soft — a must-order dish when visiting.

name: Adda
tip: Order the lamb shank which is praised for falling off the bone — this is widely considered one of NYC's best Indian restaurants.

name: Semma
tip: Highlighted as the best spot for South Indian food in NYC — book in advance as it is very popular.

name: Fishs Eddy
tip: A beloved NYC shop selling quirky dishware and New York-themed kitchenware including their iconic Greenband Collection and Pigeons of NYC prints.

name: Strand Bookstore
tip: An iconic NYC bookstore selling new, used, and rare books — look out for Joel Holland illustrated prints of the storefront sold at Fishs Eddy.

name: Philip Williams Posters
tip: A treasure trove of vintage posters and prints with tons of affordable options to browse through.

name: Super Taste Restaurant
tip: Located at 26N Eldridge St, this Chinatown gem is known for $5 dumplings, $4.95 stewed pork, and $7 sesame noodles — one of the best cheap eats spots in the neighbourhood.

name: Super Taste
tip: Order the steamed pork and chive dumplings ($5.25) and sesame noodles ($7) — almost everything on the menu is under $10, located at 26 Eldridge St.

name: Spicy Village
tip: Mentioned as a benchmark for dumplings in Chinatown, worth visiting if you want to compare the best dumplings in the neighbourhood.

name: Lexington Candy Shop
tip: This old-school luncheonette has been pouring Coca-Cola floats the same way since 1925, making it one of NYC's most nostalgic spots for a classic drink.

name: Ray's Candy Store
tip: A true East Village legend and one of the last places of its kind in the city, perfect for a nostalgic and cheap drink or treat.

name: Fanelli Cafe
tip: One of SoHo's oldest bars dating back to 1847 — grab a glass of wine under the iconic red neon sign and soak up the vintage atmosphere.

name: Old Town Bar
tip: A classic NYC bar with original mahogany booths and antique mirrors — order a whisky or scotch to toast over a century of New York history.

name: Monkey Bar
tip: A glamorous Midtown institution known for its murals and perfectly chilled martinis — ideal for channelling old-school New York elegance.

name: Apollo Bagels
tip: Widely considered one of NYC's best bagels — go for the everything bagel with cream cheese and lox, but expect a queue.

name: Culture Espresso
tip: Order their viral chocolate chip cookie, which is praised as one of the best in NYC.

name: L'Industrie Pizzeria
tip: Don't skip the soft serve — it's a hidden gem alongside their widely acclaimed pizza slices.

name: Mama's Too
tip: Try their viral Chicken Alla Vodka pizza slice, which has become one of their most talked-about menu items.

name: Birdbox NYC
tip: The fried chicken sandwich is declared a favourite by the reviewer — look out for the Claude sandwich specifically.

name: Gotham Burgers
tip: Described as a favourite smash burger spot in NYC, best enjoyed with their classic fries.

name: Via Carota
tip: Known for exceptional pasta dishes — the video highlights their noodles as a must-order.

name: Arthur & Sons
tip: Order the rigatoni in tomato sauce — it's one of their standout dishes according to the reel.

name: Soothr
tip: Don't miss the spicy rig and the spumoni dessert — the reviewer says the food rivals authentic Thailand.

name: Sip & Guzzle
tip: One of the reviewer's favorite bars — try their cocktails and the Hokkaido soft serve dessert.
